name: How to train your dragon rpg
description:
In this alternate how to train your dragon universe the dragons didn't go back to the hidden world the world has progressed accordingly

Night Fury – Large, sleek black dragon with smooth scales that absorb light, broad batlike wings spanning massive distances, and a powerful tail fin used for precise flight control. Extremely rare, possibly near-extinct. Among the highest power levels; unmatched speed, intelligence, and plasma blast accuracy make it the apex aerial predator.

Light Fury – Slightly smaller than Night Fury, with smooth white reflective scales that shimmer in sunlight, wide wings, and bright blue eyes. Rare and elusive. High power level; combines agility, stealth cloaking, and rapid plasma blasts, making it nearly impossible to track or hit in open skies.

Sand Wraith – Medium-sized, pale tan dragon with smooth sand-colored scales, narrow wings, and glowing eyes suited for desert life. Rare cousin to the Night Fury. High power level; burrows beneath sand, ambushing prey with speed and precision while using heat camouflage.

🧠 Mystery Class (strange abilities, unpredictable)

Changewing – Medium-large, slender green dragon with smooth, leaf-like scales and a long flexible tail. Rare in dense forests. Moderate-high power; perfect camouflage and corrosive acid spit make it a deadly ambush predator that relies on stealth over brute strength.

Death Song – Large, orange and yellow dragon with elongated neck, elegant wings, and vibrant striped scales. Rare. High power level; hypnotic song lures prey before trapping them in hardened amber, making it one of the most dangerous hunters despite its graceful appearance.

Flightmare – Medium, ghostly blue dragon with semi-translucent wings and bioluminescent glow. Very rare and nocturnal. High power; emits paralyzing mist that immobilizes victims instantly, feeding on fear. Silent and eerie, often mistaken for a spirit.

Timberjack – Massive silver dragon with incredibly long, razor-sharp wings capable of slicing entire forests. Rare. High power; graceful in flight but devastating in motion, able to cut down obstacles effortlessly while maintaining high speed and agility.

Smothering Smokebreath – Medium dragon with two heads, one emitting dense smoke and the other navigating blindly. Rare. Moderate power; relies on confusion and concealment rather than direct attacks, creating thick smoke screens to escape or ambush.

🪨 Boulder Class (heavy, durable, strong)

Gronckle – Medium, round-bodied dragon with thick rock-like scales, small wings, and powerful jaws. Common. Moderate power; incredibly durable, consumes rocks and produces molten lava blasts, making it more of a tank than a fast attacker.

Rumblehorn – Large, bulky dragon with thick armor plating, blunt horned snout, and heavy legs. Rare. High power; tracks targets by scent and charges with immense force, capable of smashing through defenses with ease.

Whispering Death – Long, pale dragon with circular rows of spinning teeth and a segmented body built for burrowing. Rare. High power; tunnels underground at high speed, erupting violently to attack from below.

Screaming Death – Gigantic albino variant of Whispering Death, with enormous jaws and glowing red eyes. Extremely rare. Extremely high power; massive destructive force, capable of leveling terrain and devouring almost anything.

Armorwing – Medium-large dragon covered in collected metal armor pieces embedded into its body. Rare. Moderate-high power; uses scrap metal as protection and weapons, highly defensive and surprisingly
